description.task: Implement a StringUtils class that provides three methods: reverseString() (without using StringBuilder.reverse()), isPalindrome() using the reversed string, and countVowels() by iterating the characters.
instructions: reverseString(s): convert to char[], swap from both ends meeting in middle, return new String
countVowels(s): iterate characters, count those in {a,e,i,o,u} (case-insensitive)
Do not use StringBuilder.reverse() in reverseString()
description.hint: reverseString: char[] arr=s.toCharArray(); int l=0,r=arr.length-1; while(l<r){char t=arr[l];arr[l++]=arr[r];arr[r--]=t;} return new String(arr);

description.task: Implement a WordFrequency class that counts the frequency of each word in a sentence (case-insensitive), finds the most frequent word, and returns a sorted list of words by frequency (descending, then alphabetically).
instructions: countWords: split sentence by " +" (regex), convert each token to lowercase, put into a HashMap incrementing count
mostFrequent: iterate countWords map, track max-count entry, return its key
sortedByFrequency: get countWords map, sort entries by value desc then key asc, collect keys to list
Input 'the cat sat on the mat the cat' → the=3, cat=2, sat=1, on=1, mat=1
description.hint: sortedByFrequency: List<Map.Entry> entries = new ArrayList<>(map.entrySet()); entries.sort((a,b)->b.getValue()-a.getValue()!=0?b.getValue()-a.getValue():a.getKey().compareTo(b.getKey())); then collect keys.

description.task: Implement a PatternMatcher class with a naive pattern search that returns the starting index of the first match, a method to find all match positions, and a method to replace all occurrences without using String.replace().
instructions: firstOccurrence: slide a window of pattern.length() over text; use text.substring(i, i+m).equals(pattern) where m=pattern.length()
allOccurrences: same sliding window, collect every matching start index
replaceAll: use allOccurrences, then build result by copying non-pattern segments and inserting replacement at each match; handle overlapping by advancing past each match
For text='abcabcabc' and pattern='abc': firstOccurrence=0, allOccurrences=[0,3,6], replaceAll='XXX'
Do not use String.contains(), String.indexOf(), or String.replace()
description.hint: firstOccurrence: int m=pattern.length(); for(int i=0;i<=text.length()-m;i++) if(text.substring(i,i+m).equals(pattern)) return i; return -1;
